title: Bedrooms
section_title: Adaptable Dwelling Units of Residential Occupancy
text: 1) A bedroom referred to in Sentence 3.8.5.2.(1) shall have an area not less than 11 m² having a length and a width not less than 3 m. 2) Except where the bedroom is located in the basement, a window sill, where provided, shall be installed not more than 1 000 mm above the floor.

title: Article 18
text:
Article 18 of that Regulation is replaced by the following: "18. The surfaces and dimensions of areas and rooms of a dwelling shall have a useful surface of at least 1o 13.5 m2, with no dimension less than 3 m, if it is a living area, whether it is a separate room or a combined area; 2o 3.25 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m measured between the face of the walls or between the face of a wall and the face of an incorporated wardrobe or apparatus, if it is a dining area combined with another area;
3o 7 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m measured between the faces of the walls or between the face of a wall and that of an incorporated cabinet or apparatus, if it is a single dining room; 4o 3.2 m2, with no dimension less than 2.3 m, if it is a kitchen, whether isolated or combined with other areas, except in dwellings where only one or two persons can sleep, in which case the minimum useful surface is 2.7 m2, with no dimension less than 1.7 m; 5o 9.8 m2, with no dimension less than 2.7 m, if it is the master bedroom; 6o 7 m2, with no dimension less than 2 m, if it is a chamber other than the master bedroom."

title: Article 14
section: CHAPTER II / SECTION II / SUBSECTION II

title: Article 14
text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 14 the following sections: "14.1. Despite section 14, natural second-day lighting of a room in a dwelling is permitted when the following conditions are met in addition to the requirements set out in subparagraphs (a) and (b) of paragraph 4 of section 9.7.2.3. of Division B of the Code: 1° second-day illuminated area and 1° second-day illuminated area with the glass surface providing natural lighting form parts combined within the meaning of the Code; 2° opening between the two areas shall be free from obstacles; 3° the glass surface providing natural lighting shall be at least 10% of the total surface of the combined rooms.

title: Article 23
text: These Regulations are amended by adding, after section 25, the following subsection and sections: "SECTION VII SUSTAINABLE CONSTRUCTION" 25.1. The roof structure of an uncombustible building of more than 3 floors in building height and more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units on the horizontal (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

title: Article 27.1
text: The roof structure of a building with a height of more than 3 floors and a height of more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontally (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.
